founder.heroTitle:
You have the idea.
You just need the 100 hands to build it.
founder.heroSub: CodyMaster gives non-technical founders the power to build, design, and deploy production-ready products — without writing a single line of code.

pains.title: Agencies drain your budget
pains.desc: You've been quoted $30K-50K for an MVP. Weeks of meetings. Revisions that cost extra. And the result? A product that doesn't match your vision.

pains.title: Ideas die waiting
pains.desc: By the time you find a developer, negotiate contracts, and start building — your competitor already launched. Speed is everything in startup land.

pains.title: No-code hits a wall
pains.desc: Wix, Squarespace, Bubble — great for static pages, terrible for real products. You need auth, dashboards, APIs, databases. No-code can't do that.

pains.title: You can't validate fast enough
pains.desc: Every day without a live product is a day you're guessing instead of learning. You need to test the market NOW, not in 3 months.

founder.solutionTitle: Your AI co-founder that never sleeps

solutions.benefit: Describe your idea in plain English. AI breaks it into an actionable plan.
Get a professional design system — 48 UX Laws, 1032+ patterns.
AI writes production code with tests. Not prototypes — real products.
One command to deploy. 8-gate pipeline. Automatic rollback if anything breaks.

after: You describe, AI builds exactly that

objections.q: Can AI really build production-quality products?
objections.a: Yes. Unlike ChatGPT copy-paste, CodyMaster enforces TDD, quality gates, and security scans. Every line of code is tested before deploy.

objections.q: I literally don't know any code. Will this work for me?
objections.a: That's exactly who this is for. The creator of CodyMaster is a PM who can't code either. You chat naturally — AI handles everything.

objections.q: What if I need changes later?
objections.a: Chat again. Tell AI what to change. It remembers context (cm-continuity) and updates without breaking existing functionality.

Complete tracking: Meta Pixel + CAPI, TikTok Events, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, UTM attribution.

objections.a: No. cm-content-factory generates original, SEO-optimized content with proper heading hierarchy, internal linking, and schema markup. It's not generic AI slop.

objections.a: cm-ads-tracker sets up first-touch and last-touch attribution, cross-channel deduplication, and UTM conventions. Enterprise-grade tracking.

cmo.ctaTitle:
Stop being the bottleneck.

pm.heroTitle:
AI gave you 100 hands.
But without discipline, those hands create chaos.
pm.heroSub: CodyMaster is the control system for AI-powered product development. Enforced quality gates, design consistency, and auto-generated documentation.
pm.painLabel: THE CHAOS YOU KNOW TOO WELL

pains.title: Design is different every time
pains.desc: Same brand, same project, three different styles. AI forgets your design system between sessions. Stakeholders think it's three different products.

pains.title: AI breaks what already works
pains.desc: You ask AI to add a feature. It rewrites half the codebase. Working functionality disappears. You spend the next 2 days undoing the damage.

pains.title: No docs means no self-onboarding
pains.desc: Amazing product, zero documentation. Users flood support with basic questions. Your support team is drowning because nobody can self-serve.

pains.title: You can't see what AI is doing
pains.desc: Running 3 AI agents on 5 tasks. Which one is stuck? What deployed where? How many tokens were spent? You have zero visibility.

pm.solutionTitle: Discipline for AI. Peace of mind for you.

solutions.benefit: Design system enforced automatically. Same brand, same style, every single time.
6-gate quality system. Nothing reaches production without passing tests, linting, and security.
Auto-generate complete docs from code: API reference, SOPs, user guides, personas, JTBD.
Context persists across sessions. AI remembers mistakes and never repeats them.

objections.q: Won't quality gates slow things down?
objections.a: They save time. Catching a bug in the gate takes seconds. Catching it in production takes days. Net effect: you ship FASTER, not slower.

objections.q: I'm a PM, not a dev. Is this too technical?
objections.a: CodyMaster's creator is a PM too. Everything is designed for you to chat naturally. You say what you want, AI handles the technical details.

objections.q: We use Jira/Linear for task management. Does this replace them?
objections.a: No. CodyMaster complements your tools. It handles the AI execution part — the quality, consistency, and deployment that Jira can't enforce.

pm.ctaTitle:
You've managed chaos long enough.
Time for discipline.
pm.ctaSub: From AI chaos to production-ready products with enforced quality.

guide.title: Validate faster. Ship confidently. Prove ROI instantly.
guide.desc: Product management is about reducing risk. CodyMaster removes the engineering bottleneck entirely, allowing you to spin up functional prototypes and MVPs in hours to gather real user data before committing months of your actual tech team's time.

dev.heroTitle:

pains.title: Setup déjà vu
pains.desc: Every. Single. Project. Git init, CI/CD config, test infra, linting, i18n, design tokens, deploy pipeline. You've done this 100 times. It never gets faster.

pains.desc: AI generates code fast. But no tests, no type safety, no error handling. You either rewrite it or ship and pray. Neither option is great.

pains.title: Deploy with fingers crossed
pains.desc: No staging environment. No rollback strategy. No quality gates. You push to main and check if production is still alive. Every time.

pains.title: Architecture decisions without context
pains.desc: AI doesn't understand your codebase's patterns. It generates code that conflicts with your architecture. You spend more time fixing than writing.

pains.title: QC has no automated safety net
pains.desc: QC engineers manually test everything. No automated test gates, no regression checks, no security scans built into the pipeline.

solutions.benefit: One command: design system, test infra, CI/CD, i18n, SEO, deploy pipeline. All wired up on day zero.
Tests are written BEFORE implementation. Every feature has coverage from the start.
8-gate pipeline: lint → type check → test → build → security → preview → deploy → verify. Auto-rollback on failure.
Full review lifecycle with technical rigor. Catches issues before they become production incidents.
For QC engineers: automated test gates, evidence-based verification, frontend safety checks. No deploy without passing.

objections.q: How does this work for a CTO managing a team?

claw.painTitle: Tired of rewriting rules for every agent?

pains.title: Locked into one agent
pains.desc: You built custom prompts and rules for Cursor. Now you want to try Claude Desktop. Start from scratch. All your work? Doesn't transfer.

pains.title: Config files don't transfer
pains.desc: .cursorrules, claude_desktop_config.json, .windsurfrules — every agent has a different format. Your knowledge is fragmented across incompatible files.

pains.title: No universal skill format
pains.desc: You learned to write skills for one agent. But the Claw ecosystem (OpenClaw, NemoClaw, OpenFang) needs interoperability. No standard exists.

pains.title: Switching agents = starting over
pains.desc: A new, better agent launches. You want to try it. But migration means re-creating everything. Lock-in by configuration complexity.

claw.solutionTitle: Write once. Run anywhere. The Claw way.

solutions.benefit: A universal adapter that translates your skills into any agent's format. One SKILL.md, every agent reads it.
Meta-skill that knows your entire skill library. Ask which skill to use — it routes instantly.
Chain skills into automated pipelines. Works the same across Cursor, Claude, Antigravity, OpenClaw, and more.
Universal skill template. Create once, compatible with Antigravity, Claude, Cursor, OpenClaw, NemoClaw, and OpenFang.

objections.q: I only use Cursor. Do I need this?
objections.a: Yes! Even on Cursor alone, you get 33 battle-tested skills. And if you ever want to try Claude or Antigravity, your entire skill library comes with you.
It's the ecosystem of AI agent tools that share the Open Claw interop philosophy: OpenClaw, NemoClaw, OpenFang, and others. CodyMaster speaks their language natively.

objections.q: Can I create my own skills in this format?
objections.a: Absolutely. cm-skill-mastery and skill-creator-ultra help you create custom skills that work across ALL Claw-compatible agents instantly.

claw.ctaTitle:

catalog.name: cm-brainstorm-idea
catalog.prompt: Analyze my e-commerce platform and suggest improvements
catalog.problem: Got a brilliant idea but paranoid about building the wrong thing and burning time?
catalog.solution: Multi-dimensional analysis to architect the absolute best execution strategy before hardcoding.
catalog.whatIsIt: A strategic analysis tactician acting as your Product Manager. Employs Design Thinking, TRIZ 9 Windows, and Double Diamond matrices to dissect mechanics, marketing, and business risks.
catalog.whenToUse: Exclusively before bootstrapping major epics or refactoring chaotic multi-tiered legacy systems.
catalog.whyToUse: Eviscerates the 'build fast but build wrong' illusion. Forces you into evaluating 2-3 structured strategic options (comparing Pros/Cons) rather than coding blindly.
catalog.bestPractice: Feed it extensive raw User Profile contexts. Consistently execute this prior to invoking `cm-planning`.

catalog.name: cm-planning
catalog.prompt: Plan the implementation for user authentication
catalog.problem: Whenever the AI is commanded to code raw, it violently demolishes your stable architecture?
catalog.solution: Force AI to formulate a comprehensive structural blueprint defining scope before keyboard hitting.
catalog.whatIsIt: The supreme operational orchestrator. Pushes out an architectural blueprint (`implementation_plan.md`) documenting exactly what files undergo structural mutation.
catalog.whenToUse: After the core idea hits green-light status (via `cm-brainstorm-idea`) and identically moments before physically coding.
catalog.whyToUse: Shatters monolithic requests into micro-manageable step-nodes. Pre-reviewing this architectural snapshot rescues you from infinite debugging loops late night.
catalog.bestPractice: Intensely scrutinize the `implementation_plan.md`. Command the AI to patch architectural flaws rigidly before ever approving movement to `cm-execution`.

catalog.name: cm-content-factory
catalog.prompt: Write a blog post about remote work productivity
catalog.problem: Your landing page SEO content sounds incredibly robotic, driving bounce rates up and conversions down?
catalog.solution: A psychological copywriting factory that mimics 8-figure conversion marketers flawlessly.
catalog.whatIsIt: A self-improving content engine imbued with 8+ hypnotic psychological frameworks (StoryBrand SB7, Cialdini's Persuasion, SUCCESs vectors) for elite marketing campaigns.
catalog.whenToUse: Drafting intensely persuasive content mediums: Lead generation funnels, blast emails, commercial blogs, or Facebook Ad hooks.
catalog.whyToUse: Moves away from generative dictionary-text into pure psychological maneuvers. It agitates exact user trauma points, weaponizes curiosity, and boosts global Conversion Rates (CRO).
catalog.bestPractice: Initially define the JTBD (Job-to-be-Done). Trigger the StoryBrand mode projecting your Customer strictly as the Hero, and your Brand as the Yoda-like Guide.

catalog.name: cm-ux-master
catalog.prompt: Design a dashboard for a SaaS analytics app
catalog.problem: Your interface is erratic, spacing grids clash, and the color hierarchies resemble a circus?
catalog.solution: Instantly impose absolute UI/UX design compliance inherited from 48 universal laws and elite patterns.
catalog.whatIsIt: The apex visual intelligence node. Integrates 48 strict UX Laws, thousands of component heuristics, deeply optimizing aesthetic A11y scaling and whitespace math.
catalog.whenToUse: Prototyping new UI layouts from scratch, auditing broken checkout friction streams, or elevating UI consistency globally.
catalog.whyToUse: Eradicates the dependency on highly expensive UX consultation. Acts as a draconian design gatekeeper verifying your aesthetic yields extreme polish and engagement.
catalog.bestPractice: Always pair this engine sequentially with `cm-ui-preview` to visualize its conceptualized mockups before dropping into Tailwind syntax.

catalog.name: cm-ui-preview
catalog.prompt: Preview a mobile app login screen
catalog.problem: A nightmare trying to conceptualize how an AI's code will visually render without compiling?
catalog.solution: Render immediate hyper-realistic mockups strictly locking down design specs before HTML processing.

meta.title: Get Started — CodyMaster
meta.description: Install CodyMaster on Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Codex and more. Get 33 AI skills in under 2 minutes.

startPage.heroSub: Choose your AI coding platform and get 33 skills in under 2 minutes.

steps.title: Get Claude Code
steps.description: CodyMaster runs as a Claude Code plugin. You need the Claude Code CLI installed first.
items: Works on Mac, Linux, and Windows (WSL)

steps.title: Add the Marketplace
steps.description: Register the CodyMaster plugin source so Claude Code can find it.
steps.output:
✓ Marketplace registered: tody-agent/codymaster
✓ cody-master plugin available

steps.title: Install All 33 Skills
steps.description: One command installs everything — TDD, debugging, deployment, UX, content, security and more.
steps.output:
✓ cody-master v3.4.0 installed
📦 33 skills: cm-tdd, cm-debugging, cm-planning, cm-safe-deploy...

steps.output: 🚀 Welcome to CodyMaster! Let's explore all 33 skills...

steps.title: Install Gemini CLI
steps.description: Make sure you have the Gemini CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Install CodyMaster
steps.description: Install directly from GitHub — no npm package needed.
steps.output:
✓ cody-master installed

steps.title: Use Skills

steps.description: Tell Codex to fetch and follow the install guide from GitHub.

steps.title: Use Skills

steps.title: Clone CodyMaster
steps.description: Clone the repository once to your home directory.

steps.description: Drop the SKILL.md files into your platform's rules or skills directory.

cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* ~/.windsurf/rules/

cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* ~/.cline/skills/

cp -r ~/.cody-master/skills/* .windsurf/rules/

meta.title: Our Story — CodyMaster Kit
meta.description: The real story behind CodyMaster: born from a Product Manager's pain, built through hundreds of sleepless nights.

storyPage.heroSub: Not a pitch. Not marketing. This is what actually happened.
storyPage.founderIntro: I'm not a developer. I'm a Product Manager with 10+ years of experience. Code — the thing I can't do — used to be the tallest wall between me and turning ideas into reality.

chapters.title: When AI Gave Me 100 Hands
chapters.content:
Then AI appeared. Not 2 extra hands. But 100 hands. Suddenly I — someone who couldn't write a single JavaScript function — could build a full-stack application. Landing page? Done. API? Got it. Database schema? AI handles it.

It felt like someone who'd been riding a bicycle their whole life suddenly got to ride a rocket.

But a rocket without a control system is just... a flying bomb.

chapters.title: Design Chaos: Different Every Time
chapters.content:
I started using AI for company projects. Project A: minimalist, sans-serif, 8px radius. Project B — same system, same brand: suddenly AI switched to serif fonts, round buttons, completely different palette. Project C: yet another style entirely.

Every time, a different design. Never consistent.

As a 10+ year Product Manager, this drove me insane. Not because designs were ugly — sometimes each one looked great individually. But put them side by side and it was pure chaos. Business needs consistency. Brand needs a system. Clients looked at 3 pages and thought they were 3 different companies.
chapters.quote: AI is brilliant at design. It's just terrible at remembering the rules.

chapters.title: The Week AI Deleted Everything
chapters.content:
This is the most painful lesson. I will never forget it.

There was a large SaaS project — running in production, needed a full refactor for multi-language, multi-country support. I handed it to AI. It started... fine. Converting this file, translating that string.

Then everything collapsed.

Context got too long, AI started "losing its mind." It fixed one page, broke another. Got Vietnamese working, English disappeared. And the worst part — during one refactor pass, AI deleted nearly all the existing code. An entire week of work. Gone.

I had to redo it. Attempt 1. Attempt 2. Attempt 3. Attempt 4.

Each time, fix one thing, lose another. I sat in front of the screen at 2 AM, staring at endless diff logs, asking myself why I trusted a production system to AI without protection.

I swore: This will never happen again.
chapters.quote: 4 attempts. An entire week lost. That's when I built the multi-layer protection system.

chapters.title: The Lazy Person's Philosophy
chapters.content:
I'll be honest: I'm lazy.

Not lazy about thinking — lazy about doing the same thing over and over. Every new project: set up git, configure deploy, create design system, wire i18n, write README... Every time the same. Every time exhausting.

And I realized: Any problem I've faced twice should become an automated process by the third time.

So I started packaging every painful lesson into a reusable skill. Improve it, refine it, make it reproducible. Next time anyone hits the same wall, they just call one command.
chapters.quote: A problem encountered twice should never be encountered a third time.

chapters.title: Great Products Nobody Knows About
chapters.content:
10+ years as a Product Manager at a SaaS company, one obsession haunted me: Self-onboarding.

Amazing product. Tons of features. But new users came in and... got lost. No proper docs. No user guides. No API reference. Support team drowning in repeat questions.

And when I built my own products without a writing team: documentation became torture. Finish coding, completely exhausted, zero energy left to write a README.

So cm-dockit was born — a documentation powerhouse. It reads code, understands code, and auto-generates: API docs, SOPs, user guides, persona analysis, JTBD mapping, process flows. Multi-language. SEO-optimized. Package your product into world-class documentation without writing a single word.
chapters.quote: The best product in the world is worthless if nobody knows how to use it.

chapters.title: Mission Control for 100 Hands
chapters.content:
When you run multiple AI agents, multiple projects, multiple tasks in parallel to maximize productivity... you quickly lose control. Which task is done? Which agent is running? Which deploy went to production? How many tokens have been spent?

I'm a visual person. I need to SEE everything that's happening.

Everything on one screen: pipeline progress, task queue, token tracker, event log. No digging through logs. No running CLI commands. Open it — look — know the situation instantly.
chapters.quote: If you can't see it, you can't control it.

chapters.title: Gratitude to the Community
chapters.content:
Not everything here came from my head.

I owe the community a tremendous debt.

Superpower, Loki Mode, UX Max Pro... Brilliant skills that amazing people shared with the world — I learned from them, curated them, refined them, and integrated them into the CodyMaster ecosystem. Every skill in this toolkit isn't just code — it's collective intelligence.

Thank you to everyone who shared. Truly.
chapters.quote: Standing on the shoulders of a generous community.

closing.title: So What Is CodyMaster?
closing.content:
It's not a plugin. Not a prompt template.

It's the answer from a Product Manager who can't code, after hundreds of sleepless nights, to one single question:
closing.question: "How can someone with ideas but no technical skills create world-class products — from landing pages, full-stack apps, documentation, to self-onboarding — without sacrificing quality?"
closing.punchline:
33 skills. Each skill is a lesson. Each lesson is a sleepless night.

And now, you don't have to go through those nights.

questions.q: Do I need to know how to code?
questions.a: No. CodyMaster is designed for people who can't code. You describe what you want in plain language — AI handles the rest.
Yes, CodyMaster is open source and free. You only pay for the AI provider (Gemini, Claude, etc.) you choose to use.

questions.q: Which AI agents does it work with?
questions.a: All major AI coding agents: Google Antigravity (Gemini), Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline/RooCode, GitHub Copilot, OpenClaw, and more.

questions.q: Can I use my existing design system?
questions.a: Yes! cm-ux-master reads your existing design tokens, components, and brand guidelines — then builds with them.

questions.q: How is this different from v0, Bolt, or Lovable?
questions.a: Those tools generate one-off prototypes. CodyMaster is a complete skills framework — it enforces TDD, quality gates, security scans, design consistency, documentation, and safe deployment. It's production-ready from day one.

questions.q: Can I create my own skills?
questions.a: Absolutely! Use cm-skill-mastery or skill-creator-ultra to create custom skills. The format is compatible with Antigravity, Claude, Cursor, and OpenClaw.

hero.title: Smart brain, self-learning. Not a goldfish brain.
hero.subtitle: CodyMaster is the only AI with smart memory inspired by neuroscience. Remembers past mistakes, forgets what's irrelevant, self-repairs when memory causes bugs. No repeating errors. No losing context. Doesn't get dumber over time.

problem.label: THE PROBLEM
problem.title: AI Without Memory = Disaster
problem.subtitle: Most AI tools have a "goldfish brain" — every session starts from zero.

cards.title: Repeating the Same Mistakes
cards.desc: Fixed CORS once? It'll break it the exact same way next time. Zero learning.

cards.title: Contradicting Itself
cards.desc: Session 1: "Use REST." Session 2: "Use GraphQL." No consistency, conflicting decisions.

cards.title: Burning Tokens
cards.desc: Re-explaining the same project context every session = wasting tokens and money.

tiers.label: THREE-TIER MEMORY
tiers.title:
Our brain knows what to remember,
what to forget, and what to keep forever.
tiers.subtitle: Inspired by neuroscience: different memories have different lifespans.

cards.title: Sensory Memory
cards.desc: Temporary info within 1 command: "file X has 200 lines." Forgotten immediately.
cards.analogy: 🧠 Like hearing a phone number once — gone instantly

cards.title: Working Memory
cards.desc: "Currently fixing auth, goal is deploy." Remembers what you're thinking and doing.
cards.analogy: 🧠 Like your grocery list today — needed now

cards.title: Long-Term Memory
cards.desc: "CORS error due to missing proxy." Lasting lessons — kept only if reinforced ≥ 2 times.
cards.analogy: 🧠 Like riding a bike — never forgotten

decay.label: EBBINGHAUS FORGETTING CURVE
decay.title: Forget smart, not forget everything.
decay.subtitle: Learnings not reinforced naturally expire. Reinforced ones last longer. Like the human brain.

heal.label: SELF-HEALING MEMORY
heal.title:
If memory causes bugs,
it fixes itself.
heal.subtitle: Before debugging, CodyMaster checks whether its own memory is the source of the problem.

steps.title: Bug Detected
steps.desc: Test fails, deploy errors, or unexpected behavior detected.

steps.title: Suspect Memory
steps.desc: Before blaming code, check: is outdated learning causing this?

steps.title: Investigate
steps.desc: Cross-reference learnings with actual codebase. Find conflicts.

steps.title: Heal & Update
steps.desc: Delete or update wrong memory. Log the correction. Prevent loops.

scope.label: SCOPE FILTERING
scope.title:
Load only what matters.
Save tokens, stay accurate.
scope.subtitle: Each learning is tagged (global, module:auth, file:routes.ts). Only relevant memories are loaded per task.

cta.title: Give your AI a real brain.
cta.subtitle: AI without memory = goldfish that talks. CodyMaster Brain turns AI into a colleague who remembers, learns fast, and never repeats the same mistakes.
